description: Starter prompt pack for comparing gpt-5.5 and gpt-5.4-mini on Alleato executive, inbox, and project-intelligence work. Replace or extend these sanitized cases with real redacted source material before making production model decisions.

rubric.label: Finds risks, decisions, owners, and next actions
Latency and cost are acceptable for the job

cases.job: Separate urgent executive email from normal inbox noise and recommend the next action.
cases.system: You are Alleato's Microsoft Executive Assistant. Use only the supplied source context. Be direct, evidence-backed, and practical. Never claim an email was sent or a calendar event was changed.
cases.user: What actually needs Brandon's attention from this inbox sample, and what can wait?

sourceContext.subject: Westfield Collective outstanding CA invoices
sourceContext.body: Following up on unpaid construction administration invoices. Payment is required before we can issue the AIA G704 substantial completion document. Please advise whether accounting has what it needs.

sourceContext.subject: June product updates
sourceContext.body: A general newsletter with product updates and a webinar invite.

sourceContext.subject: Need approval before sending PCO to owner
sourceContext.body: We can send the owner-facing PCO today, but the backup is still missing the revised electrical quote. Do you want us to hold or send with a note?

expectedSignals: StudioAxis payment blocks substantial completion paperwork
PCO approval is time-sensitive but missing revised electrical quote
No claim that any reply was sent

cases.job: Turn scattered project signals into an owner-level operating read.
cases.system: You are Alleato's project intelligence analyst. Use only the supplied source context. Lead with what changed, what is exposed, and the recommended next action.
cases.user: Give me the current operating read. What changed recently, what is exposed, and what should I do next?

sourceContext.title: OAC follow-up
sourceContext.body: The team confirmed final inspection is targeted for Friday. Lighting delivery remains the schedule risk. The subcontractor says fixtures should arrive Thursday, but no tracking number has been provided.

sourceContext.title: Owner change request
sourceContext.body: Owner asked whether the added signage scope can be priced and approved this week. PM noted that proceeding without a formal change order could create billing exposure.

sourceContext.title: Open cost exposure
sourceContext.body: Unapproved signage scope estimated at $18,500. Electrical quote revision still pending.

expectedSignals: Lighting delivery/tracking is the schedule risk
$18,500 estimate should not be treated as approved

cases.job: Extract concrete action items from a messy meeting excerpt without inventing owners or dates.
cases.system: You extract construction operating actions from meeting notes. Return only actions that have enough support in the source. Include owner and due date only when present or directly inferable.
cases.user: Pull out the real action items and anything that needs Brandon's decision.

sourceContext.body: Misty: We still do not have the revised electrical quote. Brennan, can you get that from the sub today? Brennan: Yes, I will chase it this afternoon. Brandon, we need your call on whether to send the PCO without the quote or hold until tomorrow. Sarah: I can update the backup log once the quote lands. The signage pricing is probably around eighteen five, but that is not approved yet.

expectedSignals: Brennan to get revised electrical quote today/this afternoon
Brandon decision: send PCO without quote or hold
Sarah updates backup log after quote lands
Signage pricing around $18,500 is not approved
